content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /job/.claude/skills/brave-search

# Brave Search

Web search and content extraction using the official Brave Search API. No browser required.

## Setup

Requires a Brave Search API account with a free subscription. A credit card is required to create the free subscription (you won't be charged).

1. Create an account at https://api-dashboard.search.brave.com/register
2. Create a "Free AI" subscription
3. Create an API key for the subscription
4. Add to your shell profile (`~/.profile` or `~/.zprofile` for zsh):
   ```bash
   export BRAVE_API_KEY="your-api-key-here"
   ```
5. Install dependencies (run once):
   ```bash
   cd skills/brave-search
   npm install
   ```

## Search

```bash
skills/brave-search/search.js "query"                         # Basic search (5 results)
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" -n 10                   # More results (max 20)
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --content               # Include page content as markdown
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --freshness pw          # Results from last week
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --freshness 2024-01-01to2024-06-30  # Date range
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --country DE            # Results from Germany
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" -n 3 --content          # Combined options
```

### Options

- `-n <num>` - Number of results (default: 5, max: 20)
- `--content` - Fetch and include page content as markdown
- `--country <code>` - Two-letter country code (default: US)
- `--freshness <period>` - Filter by time:
  - `pd` - Past day (24 hours)
  - `pw` - Past week
  - `pm` - Past month
  - `py` - Past year
  - `YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD` - Custom date range

## Extract Page Content

```bash
skills/brave-search/content.js https://example.com/article
```

Fetches a URL and extracts readable content as markdown.

## Output Format

```
--- Result 1 ---
Title: Page Title
Link: https://example.com/page
Age: 2 days ago
Snippet: Description from search results
Content: (if --content flag used)
  Markdown content extracted from the page...

--- Result 2 ---
...
```

## When to Use

- Searching for documentation or API references
- Looking up facts or current information
- Fetching content from specific URLs
- Any task requiring web search without interactive browsing


ARGUMENTS: Adam Peters Strategic Veteran

input.command: node skills/brave-search/search.js "Adam Peters Strategic Veteran" -n 10 --content 2>/dev/null | head -3000

After 13 years in the military, I faced significant challenges when trying to transition to civilian life. But then one day, I discovered my sense of purpose, my mission, **the fire that lights my soul**... and the one thing that leaves me feeling like I can't wait to wake up the next day when I lay down at night. And that’s exactly why **I started the Strategic Veteran**… to help my fellow veterans seamlessly transition into civilian life after a life of service with our in-depth and comprehensive services. From personalized career guidance, to mental health support, we’re dedicated to **helping you thrive** in your post-service journey. --- Result 3 --- Title: The Strategic Veteran - Bridging Military and Civilian Worlds Link: https://www.thestrategicveteran.com/about Snippet: The Strategic Veteran was founded by Adam Peters. Helping to curb veteran suicide is the ultimate purpose that goes far beyond business. The Strategic Veteran was founded by Adam Peters. Our mission is to empower veterans through entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship, providing them with the tools, resources, and support they need to succeed in business and life. Content: [Skip to content](#primary) July 16, 2024 ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422827275-personal_adam_peters_headshot.jpg) We caught up with the brilliant and insightful Adam Peters a few weeks ago and have shared our conversation below. ##### **Adam, thanks so much for taking the time to share your insights and lessons with us today. We’re particularly interested in hearing about how you became such a resilient person. Where do you get your resilience from?** I derive my resilience from personal experiences and a deeply ingrained mindset. Growing up in a challenging environment, I learned early on the importance of perseverance and adaptability. My time in the military further solidified these traits, where resilience wasn’t just a personal quality but a necessity for survival and success. The structured training, the camaraderie, and the high-stakes situations all contributed to building a robust mental and emotional fortitude. Additionally, building The Strategic Veteran has reinforced my resilience. It has required navigating numerous obstacles, learning from setbacks, and continuously pushing forward to create a meaningful impact for fellow veterans. It’s about facing adversity head-on, learning from each experience, and always striving to turn challenges into opportunities for growth. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422865683-adam_peters_img_4590-e1721202032213.jpg) ##### **Thanks, so before we move on maybe you can share a bit more about yourself?** My name is Adam Peters, and my story is one of radical transformation, healing, and newfound purpose. After my military service, I found myself in a dark place—overweight, directionless, and overwhelmed by a profound sense of hopelessness. I spent a lot of time feeling sorry for myself, utterly confused, and on the verge of giving up on life altogether. Then, everything changed. A pivotal moment in my journey was experiencing an Ayahuasca ceremony. This profound and life-altering experience helped me confront my deepest issues, stripping away layers of pain and confusion. I emerged with a newfound clarity and purpose that I had never felt before. This was the spark that ignited the creation of The Strategic Veteran. In just eight months, I’ve built The Strategic Veteran into a dynamic, thriving community. Our mission is to empower veterans through entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship, providing them with the tools, resources, and support they need to succeed in business and life. What excites me most is witnessing veterans discover their untapped potential, transform their lives, and create something significant. Health and wellness have also been central to my journey. Since July 7th of last year, I have shed almost 100 pounds. This physical transformation is a testament to my commitment to holistic healing and the power of purpose-driven living. A cornerstone of The Strategic Veteran is our focus on networking. We connect veterans with mentors, industry leaders, and like-minded peers who understand their unique challenges. These connections are essential for finding purpose and navigating the complex path to success. By fostering a robust network, we help veterans uncover opportunities and build impactful relationships that support their entrepreneurial and intrapreneurial journeys. We’re launching several exciting initiatives to further our mission. Our courses are designed to help veterans transition from a military mindset to a business mindset, covering critical skills like leadership, resilience, and problem-solving. Additionally, we are organizing events and workshops to strengthen our community and provide valuable learning experiences. I’m particularly excited about our one-on-one coaching sessions, which offer personalized support to veterans at various stages of their entrepreneurial ventures. These sessions are tailored to provide actionable insights and advice to help veterans overcome specific challenges and achieve their goals. The Strategic Veteran is more than just a business platform. It’s a movement dedicated to holistic healing, rediscovering purpose, and building lives filled with meaning and fulfillment. We are here to make a lasting impact, supporting veterans every step of the way as they build futures they can be proud of. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422898285-adam_peters_img_4222.png) ##### **There is so much advice out there about all the different skills and qualities folks need to develop in order to succeed in today’s highly competitive environment and often it can feel overwhelming. So, if we had to break it down to just the three that matter most, which three skills or qualities would you focus on?** Looking back, three qualities were most impactful in my journey: resilience, self-awareness, and networking. These qualities helped me navigate the challenges of transitioning from military to civilian life and --- Result 8 --- Title: The Strategic Veteran Podcast | Insights for Successful Military Transition Link: https://www.thestrategicveteran.com/podcast Snippet: Real Stories, Real Strategies, Real Success Hosted by Adam Peters ... Content: In this episode, Peter Pollock, a retired U.S. Air Force colonel, shares what really happens after a senior officer retires and the structure disappears. We talk about identity loss at the officer level, leadership myths in corporate America, why rank means nothing on the outside, and how veterans collide with mediocrity after service. This is a blunt conversation about leadership, failure, and starting over when the playbook you lived by no longer applies. Content: [Skip to content](#primary) July 16, 2024 ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422827275-personal_adam_peters_headshot.jpg) We caught up with the brilliant and insightful Adam Peters a few weeks ago and have shared our conversation below. ##### **Adam, thanks so much for taking the time to share your insights and lessons with us today. We’re particularly interested in hearing about how you became such a resilient person. Where do you get your resilience from?** I derive my resilience from personal experiences and a deeply ingrained mindset. Growing up in a challenging environment, I learned early on the importance of perseverance and adaptability. My time in the military further solidified these traits, where resilience wasn’t just a personal quality but a necessity for survival and success. The structured training, the camaraderie, and the high-stakes situations all contributed to building a robust mental and emotional fortitude. Additionally, building The Strategic Veteran has reinforced my resilience. It has required navigating numerous obstacles, learning from setbacks, and continuously pushing forward to create a meaningful impact for fellow veterans. It’s about facing adversity head-on, learning from each experience, and always striving to turn challenges into opportunities for growth. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422865683-adam_peters_img_4590-e1721202032213.jpg) ##### **Thanks, so before we move on maybe you can share a bit more about yourself?** My name is Adam Peters, and my story is one of radical transformation, healing, and newfound purpose. After my military service, I found myself in a dark place—overweight, directionless, and overwhelmed by a profound sense of hopelessness. I spent a lot of time feeling sorry for myself, utterly confused, and on the verge of giving up on life altogether. Then, everything changed. A pivotal moment in my journey was experiencing an Ayahuasca ceremony. This profound and life-altering experience helped me confront my deepest issues, stripping away layers of pain and confusion. I emerged with a newfound clarity and purpose that I had never felt before. This was the spark that ignited the creation of The Strategic Veteran. In just eight months, I’ve built The Strategic Veteran into a dynamic, thriving community. Our mission is to empower veterans through entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship, providing them with the tools, resources, and support they need to succeed in business and life. What excites me most is witnessing veterans discover their untapped potential, transform their lives, and create something significant. Health and wellness have also been central to my journey. Since July 7th of last year, I have shed almost 100 pounds. This physical transformation is a testament to my commitment to holistic healing and the power of purpose-driven living. A cornerstone of The Strategic Veteran is our focus on networking. We connect veterans with mentors, industry leaders, and like-minded peers who understand their unique challenges. These connections are essential for finding purpose and navigating the complex path to success. By fostering a robust network, we help veterans uncover opportunities and build impactful relationships that support their entrepreneurial and intrapreneurial journeys. We’re launching several exciting initiatives to further our mission. Our courses are designed to help veterans transition from a military mindset to a business mindset, covering critical skills like leadership, resilience, and problem-solving. Additionally, we are organizing events and workshops to strengthen our community and provide valuable learning experiences. I’m particularly excited about our one-on-one coaching sessions, which offer personalized support to veterans at various stages of their entrepreneurial ventures. These sessions are tailored to provide actionable insights and advice to help veterans overcome specific challenges and achieve their goals. The Strategic Veteran is more than just a business platform. It’s a movement dedicated to holistic healing, rediscovering purpose, and building lives filled with meaning and fulfillment. We are here to make a lasting impact, supporting veterans every step of the way as they build futures they can be proud of. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422898285-adam_peters_img_4222.png) ##### **There is so much advice out there about all the different skills and qualities folks need to develop in order to succeed in today’s highly competitive environment and often it can feel overwhelming. So, if we had to break it down to just the three that matter most, which three skills or qualities would you focus on?** Looking back, three qualities were most impactful in my journey: resilience, self-awareness, and networking. content.content: --- Result 1 --- Title: The Strategic Veteran | Expert Support for Military to Civilian Transition Link: https://www.thestrategicveteran.com/ Snippet: And that’s exactly why I started the Strategic Veteran… to help my fellow veterans seamlessly transition into civilian life after a life of service with our in-depth and comprehensive services. From personalized career guidance, to mental health support, we’re dedicated to helping you thrive in your post-service journey. ... Take us anywhere you go, and listen to us on your favorite podcast app so you can discover the secrets of what it takes to survive & thrive post service and even start your own successful business. Content: Hi, I'm Adam Peters. After 13 years in the military, I faced significant challenges when trying to transition to civilian life. Content: [Skip to content](#content) * * * [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/amwarriorradio/) * [Apple Podcasts](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/american-warrior-radio/id1808053409) * [RSS](https://americanwarriorradio.com/feed) * [Support American Warrior Radio](https://www.patreon.com/Americanwarriorradio) ## Heroes Media Group – Adam Bird **Heroes Media Group** Founder and CEO **[Adam Bird](https://www.heroesmediagroup.com/)** is our featured Veteran Entrepreneur for this episode.  Adam founded Heroes Media Group (A digital media branding and publishing company) to give a voice to our community heroes and those who support them. ![Heroes Media](https://americanwarriorradio.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/Bird-300x300.jpg) Adam enlisted in the Army National Guard after high school. His entry into communications and media was by accident; he “cut his teeth” in radio at KFNX in Phoenix and then began podcasting. Originally, Heroes Media Group was to be the communications arm for a non-profit called The Heroes Alliance Network for the Dutiful but it grew to take on a life of its own. Adam expanded the content of Heroes Media Group beyond the military to include other community heroes like **[law enforcement](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/police-week-sherry-graham-potter-dave-bray/)**, **[first responders](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/never-forget-tim-brown-nyfd/)**, teachers and **[clerg](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/bishop-top-gun-derek-jones/)y**. We spend some time talking about entrepreneurship and how this path is not for everyone. It can be a grind and one truly MUST have a passion for what they do. One of the services Heroes Media Group offers is podcast production and hosting. Nielson estimates there are over 700,000 podcasts in the US with more being added every week. “Pod-fade” affects many of these endeavors, with the majority of podcasts not making it past 7 episodes. We spend some time discussing the podcast phenomenon and Adam’s suggestions for persons considering breaking into this communication medium including monetization options and ways to diversity the message. One of Adam’s clients is transcribing elements of their podcast and converting it to a coffee table book. Adam’s bottom line advice; “If your heart’s not in it, you will go nowhere very quickly.” Content and willingness to adapt the message is key to a successful podcast. Adam asked his “Decision Hour” podcast listeners what THEY wanted to hear and it led to some very different but entertaining episodes. **TAKEAWAY:  “Broadcasting is not a right for us – it’s a privilege. Every combat veteran has a story to tell, we want to hear it. Every combat veteran has unique capabilities, we want to utilize them. Every combat veteran has the ability to overcome disadvantages, we want to empower them. Every combat veteran has the power to heal, we want to educate others. All Music excerpts used by permission. ## Episodes ![SOCRS127- Adam Bird- "Heroes Media Group: A Brighter Future for Podcasters"](https://pbcdn1.podbean.com/imglogo/ep-logo/pbblog2187169/adam_bird_ayq7m_300x300.jpg) Friday May 29, 2020 Friday May 29, 2020 Adam Bird is the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Heroes Media Group LLC. Heroes Media Group (HMG) is a Digital Media Branding platform committed to providing a voice for our community heroes and those who support them. HMG is committed to providing education, entertainment and empowerment to: Active Duty Service Members, Veterans, Firefighters, First Responders, Law Enforcement, Educators, Medical Professionals, and Clergy. Adam earned his Bachelors Degree in Business Management in 2011. He is an experienced entrepreneur and leader in building companies, educating, training and mentoring and empowering teams to success. Ultimately, Adam founded HMG to create jobs and reorganize the way media is consumed, delivered and trusted on a global scale. Content: ##### Newsletter _Sed ut perspiciatis unde._ [Subscribe](#) We’re excited to introduce you to the always interesting and insightful Adam Bird. We hope you’ll enjoy our conversation with Adam below. ##### **Alright, Adam thanks for taking the time to share your stories and insights with us today. Risk taking is something we’re really interested in and we’d love to hear the story of a risk you’ve taken.** I would have to say the biggest risk I’ve taken was quitting my day job, and just going all in on starting my business. I was at a job where I knew more about the job than my manager. He would pull me aside and ask questions about what we can do to bring sales up for our team and get the team to listen to him more etc. Then the next day he would call a team meeting and say everything I told him the day before. I didn’t have a problem with it at first, but then he started doing it all the time. I got sick of doing his job and I felt like I should be doing something else. As a single parent, (with full custody and no help) and no money in the bank, I decided to quit my day job to go all in on my business. I didn’t have a plan, wasn’t sure what I was going to do, I just knew I needed to make a change It wasn’t easy, and I certainly wouldn’t recommended anyone doing it. ![](https://cdn.canvasrebel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/c-AdamBird__9701405334652442468385513701384463297544192n_1656348322304.jpg) ##### **Adam, before we move on to more of these sorts of questions, can you take some time to bring our readers up to speed on you and what you do?** I got into the industry all from a radio interview where I was explaining education benefits for military members and veterans. From there I co-cofounded a company, and we went all on-line and had an internet radio show. I started learning how to edit audio, and soon people were asking me for help. After a few years I started what is now Heroes Media Group. Heroes Media Group (HMG) is a digital Media and Publishing platform committed to providing a voice for our community heroes and those who support them. We help people start their podcast and provide full editing services. We also work with authors and help them create their audiobook. ##### **Any advice for managing a team?** When it comes to managing a team and maintaining morale it’s important to have great communication with the team. Make sure everyone understands what needs to be done and when the task needs to be done by. And then get out of their way and let them do it. Also, when someone does something good, give them praises for doing a good job. ![](https://cdn.canvasrebel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/c-AdamBird__IMG6711_1656348345567.jpg) ![](https://cdn.canvasrebel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/c-AdamBird__HMGBanner_1656348376617.jpg) ##### **What’s worked well for you in terms of a source for new clients?** The best source for new clients for us is, referrals. Content: In this episode of _Wake Up with Patti Katter_, host Patti Katter welcomed back a fan-favorite guest, Adam Bird, the CEO of Heroes Media Group. Their engaging conversation was packed with laughter, nostalgia, and valuable insights into the media industry, personal growth, and the power of community. The episode began with a heartwarming look at Patti and Adam’s decade-long friendship. They shared stories of their journey together, highlighting their fun traditions, including their trademark photo poses. This personal connection set the tone for a relaxed yet impactful discussion. Adam shared exciting developments from Heroes Media Group in 2024: * **New Shows:** The network has expanded its lineup with diverse, audience-focused content. * **Revamped Website:** Heroes Media Group recently launched a redesigned website, offering an improved user experience and streamlined access to their growing content library. * **Event Highlights:** From the Army-Navy game to West Point Athletics, the network has increased its presence at key events, strengthening its ties within the military and veteran communities. A highlight of the conversation was Adam’s candid reflections on his coffee business. He discussed the challenges of sourcing quality beans amidst rising costs and the unexpected popularity of their unique blends. Beyond the product, Adam emphasized the importance of **community connections**, recounting how they engage with customers directly at local events through coffee tastings and pop-up tables. The conversation turned toward the role of mainstream media in shaping public perception. Adam voiced concerns about sensationalism taking precedence over truth and encouraged listeners to actively seek reliable information. Both Patti and Adam underscored the importance of critical thinking and forming independent opinions. Adam shared a pivotal lesson from 2024: learning to set realistic expectations as a business owner and a family man. He reflected on his efforts to be more present in his personal life, cherishing time with his family while balancing professional responsibilities. The episode concluded with a shared message of hope, resilience, and community. Patti and Adam encouraged listeners to remain engaged, support one another, and focus on values that foster personal and collective growth.

His entry into communications and media was by accident; he “cut his teeth” in radio at KFNX in Phoenix and then began podcasting. Originally, Heroes Media Group was to be the communications arm for a non-profit called The Heroes Alliance Network for the Dutiful but it grew to take on a life of its own. Adam expanded the content of Heroes Media Group beyond the military to include other community heroes like **[law enforcement](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/police-week-sherry-graham-potter-dave-bray/)**, **[first responders](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/never-forget-tim-brown-nyfd/)**, teachers and **[clerg](https://americanwarriorradio.com/awr_shows/bishop-top-gun-derek-jones/)y**. We spend some time talking about entrepreneurship and how this path is not for everyone. It can be a grind and one truly MUST have a passion for what they do. Nielson estimates there are over 700,000 podcasts in the US with more being added every week. “Pod-fade” affects many of these endeavors, with the majority of podcasts not making it past 7 episodes. We spend some time discussing the podcast phenomenon and Adam’s suggestions for persons considering breaking into this communication medium including monetization options and ways to diversity the message. We help people start their podcast and provide full editing services. We also work with authors and help them create their audiobook. ##### **Any advice for managing a team?** When it comes to managing a team and maintaining morale it’s important to have great communication with the team. Make sure everyone understands what needs to be done and when the task needs to be done by. And then get out of their way and let them do it. Also, when someone does something good, give them praises for doing a good job. ![](https://cdn.canvasrebel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/c-AdamBird__IMG6711_1656348345567.jpg) ![](https://cdn.canvasrebel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/c-AdamBird__HMGBanner_1656348376617.jpg) ##### **What’s worked well for you in terms of a source for new clients?** The best source for new clients for us is, referrals. More than 90% of the people we work with have been through referrals from other clients we’ve had. If you go above and beyond what you promise and do a great job, people will send you referrals. **Contact Info:** * **Website:** [https://www.heroesmediagroup.com](https://www.heroesmediagroup.com/) * **Instagram:** [https://www.instagram.com/heroesmediagrp/](https://www.instagram.com/heroesmediagrp/) * **Facebook:** [https://www.facebook.com/heroesmediagrp](https://www.facebook.com/heroesmediagrp) * **Linkedin:** [https://www.linkedin.com/company/heroesmediagrp](https://www.linkedin.com/company/heroesmediagrp) * **Twitter:** [https://twitter.com/HeroesMediagrp](https://twitter.com/HeroesMediagrp) * **Youtube:** [https://www.youtube.com/HeroesMediaGRP](https://www.youtube.com/HeroesMediaGRP) [Suggest a Story:](https://canvasinterviews.com/recommendations) CanvasRebel is built on recommendations from the community; it’s how we uncover hidden gems, so if you or someone you know deserves recognition please let us know [here.](https://canvasinterviews.com/recommendations) --- Result 9 --- Title: Heroes Media Group | Facebook Link: https://www.facebook.com/heroesmediagrp/ Snippet: Heroes Media Group. 5,330 likes · 193 talking about this. A highlight of the conversation was Adam’s candid reflections on his coffee business. Content: In this episode of _Wake Up with Patti Katter_, host Patti Katter welcomed back a fan-favorite guest, Adam Bird, the CEO of Heroes Media Group. Their engaging conversation was packed with laughter, nostalgia, and valuable insights into the media industry, personal growth, and the power of community. The episode began with a heartwarming look at Patti and Adam’s decade-long friendship. They shared stories of their journey together, highlighting their fun traditions, including their trademark photo poses. This personal connection set the tone for a relaxed yet impactful discussion. Adam shared exciting developments from Heroes Media Group in 2024: * **New Shows:** The network has expanded its lineup with diverse, audience-focused content. * **Revamped Website:** Heroes Media Group recently launched a redesigned website, offering an improved user experience and streamlined access to their growing content library. * **Event Highlights:** From the Army-Navy game to West Point Athletics, the network has increased its presence at key events, strengthening its ties within the military and veteran communities. A highlight of the conversation was Adam’s candid reflections on his coffee business. He discussed the challenges of sourcing quality beans amidst rising costs and the unexpected popularity of their unique blends. Beyond the product, Adam emphasized the importance of **community connections**, recounting how they engage with customers directly at local events through coffee tastings and pop-up tables. The conversation turned toward the role of mainstream media in shaping public perception. Adam voiced concerns about sensationalism taking precedence over truth and encouraged listeners to actively seek reliable information. Both Patti and Adam underscored the importance of critical thinking and forming independent opinions. Adam shared a pivotal lesson from 2024: learning to set realistic expectations as a business owner and a family man. He reflected on his efforts to be more present in his personal life, cherishing time with his family while balancing professional responsibilities. The episode concluded with a shared message of hope, resilience, and community. Patti and Adam encouraged listeners to remain engaged, support one another, and focus on values that foster personal and collective growth.

Hi, I'm Adam Peters. After 13 years in the military, I faced significant challenges when trying ... Content: Hi, I'm Adam Peters. After 13 years in the military, I faced significant challenges when trying to transition to civilian life. But then one day, I discovered my sense of purpose, my mission, **the fire that lights my soul**... and the one thing that leaves me feeling like I can't wait to wake up the next day when I lay down at night. And that’s exactly why **I started the Strategic Veteran**… to help my fellow veterans seamlessly transition into civilian life after a life of service with our in-depth and comprehensive services. From personalized career guidance, to mental health support, we’re dedicated to **helping you thrive** in your post-service journey. --- Result 4 --- Title: Meet Adam Peters - Bold Journey Magazine Link: https://boldjourney.com/meet-adam-peters/ Age: July 17, 2024 Snippet: We are here to make a lasting impact, supporting veterans every step of the way as they build futures they can be proud of. Looking back, three qualities were most impactful in my journey: resilience, self-awareness, and networking. Content: [Skip to content](#primary) July 16, 2024 ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422827275-personal_adam_peters_headshot.jpg) We caught up with the brilliant and insightful Adam Peters a few weeks ago and have shared our conversation below. ##### **Adam, thanks so much for taking the time to share your insights and lessons with us today. We’re particularly interested in hearing about how you became such a resilient person. Where do you get your resilience from?** I derive my resilience from personal experiences and a deeply ingrained mindset. Growing up in a challenging environment, I learned early on the importance of perseverance and adaptability. My time in the military further solidified these traits, where resilience wasn’t just a personal quality but a necessity for survival and success. The structured training, the camaraderie, and the high-stakes situations all contributed to building a robust mental and emotional fortitude. Additionally, building The Strategic Veteran has reinforced my resilience. It has required navigating numerous obstacles, learning from setbacks, and continuously pushing forward to create a meaningful impact for fellow veterans. It’s about facing adversity head-on, learning from each experience, and always striving to turn challenges into opportunities for growth. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422865683-adam_peters_img_4590-e1721202032213.jpg) ##### **Thanks, so before we move on maybe you can share a bit more about yourself?** My name is Adam Peters, and my story is one of radical transformation, healing, and newfound purpose. After my military service, I found myself in a dark place—overweight, directionless, and overwhelmed by a profound sense of hopelessness. I spent a lot of time feeling sorry for myself, utterly confused, and on the verge of giving up on life altogether. Then, everything changed. A pivotal moment in my journey was experiencing an Ayahuasca ceremony. This profound and life-altering experience helped me confront my deepest issues, stripping away layers of pain and confusion. I emerged with a newfound clarity and purpose that I had never felt before. This was the spark that ignited the creation of The Strategic Veteran. In just eight months, I’ve built The Strategic Veteran into a dynamic, thriving community. Our mission is to empower veterans through entrepreneurship and intrapreneurship, providing them with the tools, resources, and support they need to succeed in business and life. What excites me most is witnessing veterans discover their untapped potential, transform their lives, and create something significant. Health and wellness have also been central to my journey. Since July 7th of last year, I have shed almost 100 pounds. This physical transformation is a testament to my commitment to holistic healing and the power of purpose-driven living. A cornerstone of The Strategic Veteran is our focus on networking. We connect veterans with mentors, industry leaders, and like-minded peers who understand their unique challenges. These connections are essential for finding purpose and navigating the complex path to success. By fostering a robust network, we help veterans uncover opportunities and build impactful relationships that support their entrepreneurial and intrapreneurial journeys. We’re launching several exciting initiatives to further our mission. Our courses are designed to help veterans transition from a military mindset to a business mindset, covering critical skills like leadership, resilience, and problem-solving. Additionally, we are organizing events and workshops to strengthen our community and provide valuable learning experiences. I’m particularly excited about our one-on-one coaching sessions, which offer personalized support to veterans at various stages of their entrepreneurial ventures. These sessions are tailored to provide actionable insights and advice to help veterans overcome specific challenges and achieve their goals. The Strategic Veteran is more than just a business platform. It’s a movement dedicated to holistic healing, rediscovering purpose, and building lives filled with meaning and fulfillment. We are here to make a lasting impact, supporting veterans every step of the way as they build futures they can be proud of. ![](https://cdn.boldjourney.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/c-1719422898285-adam_peters_img_4222.png) ##### **There is so much advice out there about all the different skills and qualities folks need to develop in order to succeed in today’s highly competitive environment and often it can feel overwhelming. So, if we had to break it down to just the three that matter most, which three skills or qualities would you focus on?** Looking back, three qualities were most impactful in my journey: resilience, self-awareness, and networking. These qualities helped me navigate the challenges of transitioning from military to civilian life and --- Result 5 --- Title: Adam Peters on Veteran Transition, Identity & Purpose | VET S.O.S. Content: AboutPressCopyrightContact usCreatorsAdvertiseDevelopersTermsPrivacyPolicy & SafetyHow YouTube worksTest new featuresNFL Sunday Ticket© 2026 Google LLC --- Result 8 --- Title: Interview with Adam Peters: Empowering Veterans through Strategic Community Building - Umbrella Local Heroes Link: https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/interview-with-adam-peters-empowering-veterans-through-strategic-community-building/ Age: July 30, 2024 Snippet: Strategic Veteran offers a paid community, one-on-one coaching, and a podcast. “Our main revenue driver is the community,” Adam says. “We offer networking opportunities, access to a group chat, and onboarding processes to help members make the most of the community.” ... Adam’s approach is holistic, addressing not only the professional but also the mental and emotional aspects of transitioning. Content: ![](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Del-Cromartie.jpg) By Del Cromartie on July 30, 2024 ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Empowering-your-transition-to-civilian-life.jpg) Willacoochee, GA – In veteran-owned businesses, few names shine as brightly as Adam Peters. As the founder of Strategic Veteran, Adam has dedicated his life to helping fellow veterans transition successfully into civilian life. His innovative approach combines community building, coaching, and mentorship, making him an industry leader in the veteran support sector. **A Journey Rooted in Service** ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Adam-Peters-Empowering-transition-to-civilian-life.jpg) Adam Peters’ story begins in a small town in southern Indiana where he was born and raised. After graduating high school, Adam joined the Army, seeking adventure and a way out of small-town life. “The Army was my ticket out,” he recalls. “It opened my eyes to a world of possibilities and different perspectives.” During his 13 years of active duty, Adam experienced firsthand the challenges of military life and the difficulties of transitioning back to civilian life. These experiences fueled his passion for helping other veterans navigate their transitions. “I’ve learned more in my travels than I ever could have if I’d stayed in one place,” he says. **Building Strategic Veteran** Adam founded Strategic Veteran in November 2023 with a clear mission: to provide veterans with a supportive community and the necessary resources to succeed in civilian life. “The name came from interactions and engagements with other veterans on LinkedIn,” Adam explains. “It resonated with people, and I ran with it.” Strategic Veteran offers a paid community, one-on-one coaching, and a podcast. “Our main revenue driver is the community,” Adam says. “We offer networking opportunities, access to a group chat, and onboarding processes to help members make the most of the community.” **A Holistic Approach to Transition** Adam’s approach is holistic, addressing not only the professional but also the mental and emotional aspects of transitioning. “Our goal is to change the military mindset to a more growth-oriented, business-focused one,” he explains. “We offer courses on marketing, copywriting, and graphic design, and we provide one-on-one coaching to address specific needs.” One of the key components of Strategic Veteran is The Stronghold, a community within the community that focuses on mental health and personal growth. “We launched The Stronghold officially on August 1st,” Adam says. “It’s about giving veterans the tools they need to succeed, whether with business advice or help with mental health.” **Impacting Lives** ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Adam-Peters-Empowering-your-transition-to-civilian-life-220x300.jpg) Adam’s work has already had a profound impact on many veterans. “Before I released the first episode of the podcast, I was contacted by a veteran who was struggling,” Adam recalls. “He said that listening to our conversations gave him the perspective he needed to seek help. That’s the kind of impact we’re aiming for.” **Looking Ahead** The future of Strategic Veteran looks bright. Adam plans to expand the community, add more courses, and bring experts to provide specialized knowledge. “We’re continuously gathering feedback from our members to improve and grow,” he says. “We want to be the go-to resource for veterans looking to transition successfully.” Adam Peters is more than just a business owner; he’s a mentor, a coach, and a symbol of hope for many veterans. If you or someone you know is a veteran struggling with the transition to civilian life, Strategic Veteran is here to help. Join the community, access valuable resources, and connect with like-minded individuals. Together, we can make the transition smoother and more successful. **Contact:** 912-656-1917 Email: [support@thestrategicveteran.com](mailto:support@thestrategicveteran.com) Website: [thestrategicveteran.com](http://thestrategicveteran.com/) --- Result 9 --- Title: The Strategic Veteran | Podcast on Spotify Link: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd Snippet: Listen to The Strategic Veteran on Spotify. Welcome to The Strategic Veteran, a raw and unfiltered journey into military transition. Host Adam Peters challenges the status quo by sharing authentic stories of veterans navigating life after service ... Content: In this episode, Peter Pollock, a retired U.S. Air Force colonel, shares what really happens after a senior officer retires and the structure disappears. After 24 years of service, command roles, deployments, and Pentagon work, Peter expected the transition to be manageable. It wasn’t. We talk about identity loss at the officer level, leadership myths in corporate America, why rank means nothing on the outside, and how veterans collide with mediocrity after service. This is a blunt conversation about leadership, failure, and starting over when the playbook you lived by no longer applies. Subscribe for real transition stories without bullshit: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up and choosing the Air Force • Being denied flight school and adapting early • Command, deployments, and Pentagon work • Retiring after 24 years and losing structure • Why corporate leadership frustrates veterans • Leadership vs title when failure actually matters • Rebuilding purpose without rank or uniform GUEST BIO Peter Pollock is a retired U.S. Air Force colonel, leadership coach, and founder of Gladius Consulting. After 24 years of service, Peter now works with leaders and organizations to build decision-making, accountability, and real leadership grounded in responsibility, not position. LINKS • Gladius Consulting: https://gladiusconsultingtx.com • Email Peter: peter@gladiusconsultingtx.com • The Strategic Veteran Podcast: • Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd • Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436 E Feb 23 1 hr 7 min In this episode, Joshua Palassis shares what it feels like when your entire plan collapses overnight. After failing aircrew selection, Joshua lost his military path, his income, and his independence and was forced to move back home and repay the Army. No backup plan. No safety net. Just failure staring him in the face. Instead of staying stuck, Joshua rebuilt from the ground up. Through self-education, entrepreneurship, relationship-building, and brutal self-honesty, he carved a new path in branding, marketing, and connection-driven business. This conversation breaks down the reality of transition, investing in yourself, finding mentors, and why people—not job titles—are the real leverage point after the military. Subscribe for real transition stories that don’t sugarcoat the process: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up in Hamilton, Ontario • Joining the Canadian military and commissioning as an infantry officer • The reality of officer life and broken expectations • Failing aircrew selection and losing a career path • Paying back the military and moving home • Investing $5,000 into self-education • Learning marketing, ClickFunnels, and online business • The power of mentorship and relationships • Becoming a connector instead of chasing titles GUEST BIO Joshua Palassis is a Canadian Army veteran, entrepreneur, brand strategist, and podcast host of The Connector. After a difficult military transition, Joshua rebuilt his life through self-education, marketing, and relationship-driven business. Today, he helps founders, executives, and creators grow their brands, land stages, and build meaningful opportunities through connection. LINKS • Joshua on Instagram: https://instagram.com/palassis • The Connector Podcast • Brand Alchemy • The Strategic Veteran Podcast: • Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd • Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436 E Feb 23 1 hr In this episode, Rand Timmerman, a Vietnam War Marine, former mustang officer, JAG attorney, and lifelong alcoholic in recovery, shares one of the most honest stories ever told on this show. From rural poverty and combat in Vietnam to decades of buried trauma, night terrors, and alcohol-fueled survival, Rand walks us through what happens when the past finally demands to be faced. At nearly seventy years old, sober and grieving deep loss, Rand made a radical choice. He walked the Appalachian Trail alongside his brother. Twenty-two hundred miles. One step at a time. Not to escape his demons, but to meet them head-on. This conversation is about war, grief, faith, addiction, brotherhood, and the truth that it is never too late to change course. Subscribe for real transition stories that don’t pull punches: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up in rural poverty and enlisting during Vietnam • Infantry combat, helicopters, bayonets, and survivor’s guilt • Alcohol as a lifelong coping mechanism • Becoming a mustang officer and Marine JAG • Night terrors, PTSD, and a psych ward at 65 • Sobriety and faith later in life • Walking the Appalachian Trail at nearly 70 • Why healing is one hard mile at a time GUEST BIO Rand Timmerman is a Vietnam War Marine veteran, former mustang officer, retired attor --- Result 10 --- Title: Endure By Faith Link: https://www.ebfllc.org/partners/podcasts Snippet: The hosts engage in candid conversations that address the challenges and opportunities faced by veterans, including topics such as national security, mental health, and the social dynamics within military communities. ​The Strategic Veteran Podcast, hosted by Army veteran and entrepreneur ... Content: ![](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Del-Cromartie.jpg) By Del Cromartie on July 30, 2024 ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Empowering-your-transition-to-civilian-life.jpg) Willacoochee, GA – In veteran-owned businesses, few names shine as brightly as Adam Peters. As the founder of Strategic Veteran, Adam has dedicated his life to helping fellow veterans transition successfully into civilian life. His innovative approach combines community building, coaching, and mentorship, making him an industry leader in the veteran support sector. **A Journey Rooted in Service** ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Adam-Peters-Empowering-transition-to-civilian-life.jpg) Adam Peters’ story begins in a small town in southern Indiana where he was born and raised. After graduating high school, Adam joined the Army, seeking adventure and a way out of small-town life. “The Army was my ticket out,” he recalls. “It opened my eyes to a world of possibilities and different perspectives.” During his 13 years of active duty, Adam experienced firsthand the challenges of military life and the difficulties of transitioning back to civilian life. These experiences fueled his passion for helping other veterans navigate their transitions. “I’ve learned more in my travels than I ever could have if I’d stayed in one place,” he says. **Building Strategic Veteran** Adam founded Strategic Veteran in November 2023 with a clear mission: to provide veterans with a supportive community and the necessary resources to succeed in civilian life. “The name came from interactions and engagements with other veterans on LinkedIn,” Adam explains. “It resonated with people, and I ran with it.” Strategic Veteran offers a paid community, one-on-one coaching, and a podcast. “Our main revenue driver is the community,” Adam says. “We offer networking opportunities, access to a group chat, and onboarding processes to help members make the most of the community.” **A Holistic Approach to Transition** Adam’s approach is holistic, addressing not only the professional but also the mental and emotional aspects of transitioning. “Our goal is to change the military mindset to a more growth-oriented, business-focused one,” he explains. “We offer courses on marketing, copywriting, and graphic design, and we provide one-on-one coaching to address specific needs.” One of the key components of Strategic Veteran is The Stronghold, a community within the community that focuses on mental health and personal growth. “We launched The Stronghold officially on August 1st,” Adam says. “It’s about giving veterans the tools they need to succeed, whether with business advice or help with mental health.” **Impacting Lives** ![Empowering your transition to civilian life](https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/Adam-Peters-Empowering-your-transition-to-civilian-life-220x300.jpg) Adam’s work has already had a profound impact on many veterans. “Before I released the first episode of the podcast, I was contacted by a veteran who was struggling,” Adam recalls. “He said that listening to our conversations gave him the perspective he needed to seek help. That’s the kind of impact we’re aiming for.” **Looking Ahead** The future of Strategic Veteran looks bright. Adam plans to expand the community, add more courses, and bring experts to provide specialized knowledge. “We’re continuously gathering feedback from our members to improve and grow,” he says. “We want to be the go-to resource for veterans looking to transition successfully.” Adam Peters is more than just a business owner; he’s a mentor, a coach, and a symbol of hope for many veterans. If you or someone you know is a veteran struggling with the transition to civilian life, Strategic Veteran is here to help. Join the community, access valuable resources, and connect with like-minded individuals. Together, we can make the transition smoother and more successful. **Contact:** 912-656-1917 Email: [support@thestrategicveteran.com](mailto:support@thestrategicveteran.com) Website: [thestrategicveteran.com](http://thestrategicveteran.com/) --- Result 9 --- Title: The Strategic Veteran | Podcast on Spotify Link: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd Snippet: Listen to The Strategic Veteran on Spotify. Welcome to The Strategic Veteran, a raw and unfiltered journey into military transition. Host Adam Peters challenges the status quo by sharing authentic stories of veterans navigating life after service ... Content: In this episode, Peter Pollock, a retired U.S. Air Force colonel, shares what really happens after a senior officer retires and the structure disappears. After 24 years of service, command roles, deployments, and Pentagon work, Peter expected the transition to be manageable. It wasn’t. We talk about identity loss at the officer level, leadership myths in corporate America, why rank means nothing on the outside, and how veterans collide with mediocrity after service. This is a blunt conversation about leadership, failure, and starting over when the playbook you lived by no longer applies. Subscribe for real transition stories without bullshit: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up and choosing the Air Force • Being denied flight school and adapting early • Command, deployments, and Pentagon work • Retiring after 24 years and losing structure • Why corporate leadership frustrates veterans • Leadership vs title when failure actually matters • Rebuilding purpose without rank or uniform GUEST BIO Peter Pollock is a retired U.S. Air Force colonel, leadership coach, and founder of Gladius Consulting. After 24 years of service, Peter now works with leaders and organizations to build decision-making, accountability, and real leadership grounded in responsibility, not position. LINKS • Gladius Consulting: https://gladiusconsultingtx.com • Email Peter: peter@gladiusconsultingtx.com • The Strategic Veteran Podcast: • Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd • Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436 E Feb 23 1 hr 7 min In this episode, Joshua Palassis shares what it feels like when your entire plan collapses overnight. After failing aircrew selection, Joshua lost his military path, his income, and his independence and was forced to move back home and repay the Army. No backup plan. No safety net. Just failure staring him in the face. Instead of staying stuck, Joshua rebuilt from the ground up. Through self-education, entrepreneurship, relationship-building, and brutal self-honesty, he carved a new path in branding, marketing, and connection-driven business. This conversation breaks down the reality of transition, investing in yourself, finding mentors, and why people—not job titles—are the real leverage point after the military. Subscribe for real transition stories that don’t sugarcoat the process: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up in Hamilton, Ontario • Joining the Canadian military and commissioning as an infantry officer • The reality of officer life and broken expectations • Failing aircrew selection and losing a career path • Paying back the military and moving home • Investing $5,000 into self-education • Learning marketing, ClickFunnels, and online business • The power of mentorship and relationships • Becoming a connector instead of chasing titles GUEST BIO Joshua Palassis is a Canadian Army veteran, entrepreneur, brand strategist, and podcast host of The Connector. After a difficult military transition, Joshua rebuilt his life through self-education, marketing, and relationship-driven business. Today, he helps founders, executives, and creators grow their brands, land stages, and build meaningful opportunities through connection. LINKS • Joshua on Instagram: https://instagram.com/palassis • The Connector Podcast • Brand Alchemy • The Strategic Veteran Podcast: • Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd • Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436 E Feb 23 1 hr In this episode, Rand Timmerman, a Vietnam War Marine, former mustang officer, JAG attorney, and lifelong alcoholic in recovery, shares one of the most honest stories ever told on this show. From rural poverty and combat in Vietnam to decades of buried trauma, night terrors, and alcohol-fueled survival, Rand walks us through what happens when the past finally demands to be faced. At nearly seventy years old, sober and grieving deep loss, Rand made a radical choice. He walked the Appalachian Trail alongside his brother. Twenty-two hundred miles. One step at a time. Not to escape his demons, but to meet them head-on. This conversation is about war, grief, faith, addiction, brotherhood, and the truth that it is never too late to change course. Subscribe for real transition stories that don’t pull punches: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd EPISODE HIGHLIGHTS • Growing up in rural poverty and enlisting during Vietnam • Infantry combat, helicopters, bayonets, and survivor’s guilt • Alcohol as a lifelong coping mechanism • Becoming a mustang officer and Marine JAG • Night terrors, PTSD, and a psych ward at 65 • Sobriety and faith later in life • Walking the Appalachian Trail at nearly 70 • Why healing is one hard mile at a time GUEST BIO Rand Timmerman is a Vietnam War Marine veteran, former mustang officer, retired attor --- Result 10 --- Title: Endure By Faith Link: https://www.ebfllc.org/partners/podcasts Snippet: The hosts engage in candid conversations that address the challenges and opportunities faced by veterans, including topics such as national security, mental health, and the social dynamics within military communities. ​The Strategic Veteran Podcast, hosted by Army veteran and entrepreneur ... Content: ## Turn AI Into a Strategic Advantage Fractional Chief AI Officer Leadership with a Proven Operating Model We step in as your fractional CAIO and implement a 90-day enterprise AI operating model built on governance, adoption, and measurable ROI. * ROI Tracking in 90 Days * [IACAIO Accredited](https://www.iacaio.org/) * No Hype.Structured Systems. ![Enterprise AI strategy consulting session from ChiefAIOfficer.com with executives reviewing AI governance and operating model framework](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/2026-chiefaiofficer-map-framework-manufacturing-professional-services-construction-scaled.webp "2026-chiefaiofficer-map-framework-manufacturing-professional-services-construction") ### **See** Verified Clients & Partners **and** [**Media Appearances**](https://chiefaiofficer.com/verified/media/) ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Tritium-Partners-logo-TM2.png "Tritium-Partners-logo---TM2") ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/8818fc.png "8818fc") ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/DEB_R_Large_2C_PMS.png "DEB_R_Large_2C_PMS") ### The Problem ## "Do Something with AI" Pressure Mid-market leaders are under immense pressure to adopt AI, but without clear direction, these initiatives often fail to deliver meaningful returns. #### Stalled Pilots Most organizations run AI pilots—but many never reach measurable ROI because teams lack a repeatable operating model, governance, and KPI ownership. That’s why we anchor every cycle to CFO-ready measurement and a clear decision gate. #### CFO Skepticism CFOs see spend, not savings. Without measurable outcomes, AI looks like an expensive experiment rather than a strategic investment. #### Wrong Priorities Efforts focus on flashy but low-impact projects like generic chatbots, missing the operational wins that actually move the needle for the business. #### No Framework Without a structured approach, AI becomes an ongoing expense, not a competitive advantage. You need a system, not just tools. ## How Do You Build Sustainable AI Capability Without Consultant Dependency? Mid-market companies don’t fail at AI because of technology. They fail because they lack ownership, governance, and a repeatable operating model. Sustainable AI capability requires structure, accountability, and internal leadership—not vendor dependency. #### Executive Ownership AI must sit at the executive level with clear decision rights, reporting cadence, and board visibility. #### Operating Model AI adoption must follow a repeatable operating cadence with defined governance checkpoints, KPI ownership, and pilot-to-scale progression. #### Financial Discipline Every AI initiative is tied to measurable ROI, defined cost accounting, and structured decision gates before scale. ### CORPORATE AI TRANSFORMATION ## The M.A.P.™ Framework: A Repeatable AI Operating Model Mid-market companies don’t need more AI experiments. They need a repeatable executive operating system. Our 90-day cycles deliver tangible, measurable results that compound over time. The M.A.P. Framework is a structured 90-day executive AI operating model built around governance, ROI discipline, and repeatable scale. The key is doing three things in order: **build the operating model**, **measure what works**, then **scale performance with CFO-ready reporting**. #### Model (Day 1-30) * Executive AI Council chartered with defined decision rights * Governance framework documented and approved * Initial pilots selected through ROI-based prioritization #### Assess (Day 31-60) * Pilots tested in controlled production environments * Performance measured against defined KPIs * Go / No-Go decision gates tied to measurable ROI #### Perform (Day 61-90) * Pilots fully deployed into operational workflows * Performance monitored through structured governance checkpoints * CFO-ready ROI reporting delivered with board-level visibility #### Proof artifacts (examples): * Executive KPI scorecard (redacted) * AI governance and use policy framework * 90-day MAP cycle executive briefing overview ## Why It Works #### No Jargon We train, build, and measure using plain language. Your team doesn't need to be AI experts. #### Fixed-Fee Pricing Total investment known up front. No surprise bills. Budget confidently. #### Proof or Pause Measurable ROI every cycle means you only continue when value is demonstrated. #### Sustainable Change Your people own the AI systems we build. Lasting capability, not consultant dependency. ## Embedded Executive AI Leadership We don’t deliver slide decks and disappear. Our Enterprise Plan embeds a fractional Chief AI Officer into your operating model—owning governance, execution cadence, and measurable outcomes across every 90-day cycle. * Fractional CAIO Executive-level AI leadership embedded within your organization—delivering measurable outcomes in quarters, not years. 2-4 weeks to first pilo --- Result 3 --- Title: Home | 42 Consulting.AI Link: https://www.42consulting.ai Snippet: Helping enterprises execute AI strategy — and helping consultancies, product companies, and solution providers build, sell, and deliver AI offerings. Content: New AI memos from the Trump administration prompt federal agencies to establish chief AI officers and OMB to launch a new CAIO AI Council. Federal agencies are appointing chief artificial intelligence officers as part of the collective movement to meet directives established in former President Joe Biden’s 2023 [AI executive order](https://governmentciomedia.com/white-house-aims-cut-ai-risks-national-security-new-regulations) and solidified by [an AI memo](https://govciomedia.com/white-house-releases-new-memos-on-ai-acquisition-innovation/) from the Trump administration.  The 2023 executive order tasked agencies with a series of responsibilities connected to the technology. It marked the first time federal agencies were mandated to appoint a designated CAIO, whose responsibilities include“coordinating their agency’s use of AI, promoting AI innovation, \[and\] managing risks from the use of AI,” according to the Office of Management and Budget’s guidance.  President Donald Trump’s April 2025 [memo on accelerating AI innovation](https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/M-25-21-Accelerating-Federal-Use-of-AI-through-Innovation-Governance-and-Public-Trust.pdf) continued efforts to establish chief AI officers and tasked the OMB director with creating a CAIO AI Council within 90 days of the memo’s release. The council will coordinate AI development and use across agencies, ensure compliance with relevant policies, promote shared tools and best practices and will automatically sunset five years after its creation unless extended by the OMB director.  Here are some of the most prominent leaders at the agency level in charge of AI:  ### White House: David Sacks Before returning to the White House, Trump [indicated](https://www.presidency.ucsb.edu/documents/statement-president-elect-donald-j-trump-announcing-the-appointment-david-o-sacks-white) he would make David Sacks his “White House AI and crypto czar.” Sacks, officially the [special advisor for AI and crypto](https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/Memo-David-Sacks-3.5.2025-1.pdf), has been influential in the White House’s AI policy initiatives including [America’s AI Action Plan.](https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/2025/07/white-house-unveils-americas-ai-action-plan/) Since Sacks did not step down from his role at Craft Ventures, the venture capital firm he co-founded, he is considered a “special government employee,” which limits him to serving in his federal capacity for only 130 days each year. ### **Agriculture Department: Christopher Alvares**  Christopher Alvares was selected as the Department of Agriculture’s CAIO in 2023. Alvares also serves as the department’s chief data officer. He helped develop the USDA’s inaugural AI strategy for FY 2025-26.  “Our progress in advanced analytics and data informed decision-making over the past decade demonstrates the importance of this inaugural AI Strategy, which will establish the technological and cultural infrastructure to responsibly use AI, increase employee satisfaction, and expand the impact of our mission delivery,” said Alvares in the [strategy’s introduction](https://www.usda.gov/sites/default/files/documents/fy-2025-2026-usda-ai-strategy.pdf).  As CDO and CAIO, Alvares is responsible for developing strategies that enable USDA to fully leverage its data as a strategic asset, improving organizational decision-making and outcomes for citizens. Alvares began his career working for private sector biotech companies, before becoming director of a data analytics team that provided genomic analytics services to other biotechnology and pharmaceutical clients.  ### **Commerce Department: Brian Epley**  [Brian Epley](https://www.linkedin.com/in/brianepley/) is the current CAIO and CIO at the Department of Commerce, according to the department’s [AI compliance plan](https://www.commerce.gov/sites/default/files/2025-09/DOC-AI-Strategy-Plan-for-OMB-Memorandum-M-25-21.pdf) released September 2025.  Epley was previously at the Department of Energy, where he served as the principal deputy CIO. He assisted the CIO in the formation of the office’s strategic direction for the protection and modernization of IT, cybersecurity, data usage and digitization.  ### **Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency: Unknown**  Lisa Einstein, who was named CISA’s first CAIO in August 2024, had been leading AI efforts since 2023 as the agency’s senior advisor for AI as well as the executive director of the Cybersecurity Advisory Committee. While at CISA, Einstein worked to help prioritize security in developing and deploying AI tools.  Einstein resigned as CAIO in February 2025.  ### **War Department: Cameron Stanley**  Cameron Stanley is the chief digital and artificial intelligence officer (CDAO) for the Department of War. Previously, Stanley served as national security transformation lead for Amazon Web Services and was the chief data officerfor the undersecretary of defense for intell --- Result 5 --- Title: AI Consultant Certification Link: https://www.chiefaiofficer.com/ai-consulting-certification-2025 Snippet: Earn your "Certified AI Consultant" credential with a "Fractional CAIO" designation Content: (HTTP 404) --- Result 6 --- Title: Chief AI Officer (CAIO) Leading Enterprise AI Transformation Link: https://www.udemy.com/course/chief-ai-officer-caio-leading-enterprise-ai-transformation/ Age: 1 month ago Snippet: You’ll also explore how to evaluate AI maturity, manage cross-functional teams, and foster a data-driven culture that accelerates innovation while maintaining transparency and fairness. Content: ## Frequently Asked Questions Find answers to common questions about our services and process. ### What types of businesses do you work with? We primarily work with small to mid-sized businesses (50-500 employees) looking to leverage AI strategically. Our clients span healthcare, manufacturing, professional services, and more. ### How long does an AI project typically take? All engagements start with our AI assessment which takes roughly four to five weeks. ### What does the first conversation look like? It's a casual 30-minute chat to understand your business, your goals, and whether AI makes sense for your situation. No preparation needed, no pressure. We'll give you an honest assessment of next steps. ### How do you ensure the security of our data? All engagements start with a mutual NDA. Your data is handled on secure infrastructure and never used to train AI models. We're happy to work with your legal or security team on specific requirements.

Content: ## Turn AI Into a Strategic Advantage Fractional Chief AI Officer Leadership with a Proven Operating Model We step in as your fractional CAIO and implement a 90-day enterprise AI operating model built on governance, adoption, and measurable ROI. * ROI Tracking in 90 Days * [IACAIO Accredited](https://www.iacaio.org/) * No Hype.Structured Systems. ![Enterprise AI strategy consulting session from ChiefAIOfficer.com with executives reviewing AI governance and operating model framework](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/2026-chiefaiofficer-map-framework-manufacturing-professional-services-construction-scaled.webp "2026-chiefaiofficer-map-framework-manufacturing-professional-services-construction") ### **See** Verified Clients & Partners **and** [**Media Appearances**](https://chiefaiofficer.com/verified/media/) ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Tritium-Partners-logo-TM2.png "Tritium-Partners-logo---TM2") ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/8818fc.png "8818fc") ![](https://chiefaiofficer.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/DEB_R_Large_2C_PMS.png "DEB_R_Large_2C_PMS") ### The Problem ## "Do Something with AI" Pressure Mid-market leaders are under immense pressure to adopt AI, but without clear direction, these initiatives often fail to deliver meaningful returns. #### Stalled Pilots Most organizations run AI pilots—but many never reach measurable ROI because teams lack a repeatable operating model, governance, and KPI ownership. That’s why we anchor every cycle to CFO-ready measurement and a clear decision gate. #### CFO Skepticism CFOs see spend, not savings. Without measurable outcomes, AI looks like an expensive experiment rather than a strategic investment. #### Wrong Priorities Efforts focus on flashy but low-impact projects like generic chatbots, missing the operational wins that actually move the needle for the business. #### No Framework Without a structured approach, AI becomes an ongoing expense, not a competitive advantage. You need a system, not just tools. ## How Do You Build Sustainable AI Capability Without Consultant Dependency? Mid-market companies don’t fail at AI because of technology. They fail because they lack ownership, governance, and a repeatable operating model. Sustainable AI capability requires structure, accountability, and internal leadership—not vendor dependency. #### Executive Ownership AI must sit at the executive level with clear decision rights, reporting cadence, and board visibility. #### Operating Model AI adoption must follow a repeatable operating cadence with defined governance checkpoints, KPI ownership, and pilot-to-scale progression. #### Financial Discipline Every AI initiative is tied to measurable ROI, defined cost accounting, and structured decision gates before scale. ### CORPORATE AI TRANSFORMATION ## The M.A.P.™ Framework: A Repeatable AI Operating Model Mid-market companies don’t need more AI experiments. They need a repeatable executive operating system. Our 90-day cycles deliver tangible, measurable results that compound over time. The M.A.P. Framework is a structured 90-day executive AI operating model built around governance, ROI discipline, and repeatable scale. The key is doing three things in order: **build the operating model**, **measure what works**, then **scale performance with CFO-ready reporting**. #### Model (Day 1-30) * Executive AI Council chartered with defined decision rights * Governance framework documented and approved * Initial pilots selected through ROI-based prioritization #### Assess (Day 31-60) * Pilots tested in controlled production environments * Performance measured against defined KPIs * Go / No-Go decision gates tied to measurable ROI #### Perform (Day 61-90) * Pilots fully deployed into operational workflows * Performance monitored through structured governance checkpoints * CFO-ready ROI reporting delivered with board-level visibility #### Proof artifacts (examples): * Executive KPI scorecard (redacted) * AI governance and use policy framework * 90-day MAP cycle executive briefing overview ## Why It Works #### No Jargon We train, build, and measure using plain language. Your team doesn't need to be AI experts. #### Fixed-Fee Pricing Total investment known up front. No surprise bills. Budget confidently. #### Proof or Pause Measurable ROI every cycle means you only continue when value is demonstrated. #### Sustainable Change Your people own the AI systems we build. Lasting capability, not consultant dependency. ## Embedded Executive AI Leadership We don’t deliver slide decks and disappear. Our Enterprise Plan embeds a fractional Chief AI Officer into your operating model—owning governance, execution cadence, and measurable outcomes across every 90-day cycle. * Fractional CAIO Executive-level AI leadership embedded within your organization—delivering measurable outcomes in quarters, not years. 2-4 weeks to first pilo --- Result 3 --- Title: Home | 42 Consulting.AI Link: https://www.42consulting.ai Snippet: Helping enterprises execute AI strategy — and helping consultancies, product companies, and solution providers build, sell, and deliver AI offerings.
